mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already below its recent peak, discount rates ease and equity risk premia compress, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive. The signal fails if rate cuts coincide with recessionary labor deterioration and equities still sell off.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with declining FEDFUNDS do not show higher 20d SPX returns than non-declining periods, or if the triggered long edge turns negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and demand, which reduces earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when unemployment is rising rather than merely high.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise episodes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in a majority of cases or if the average forward return is not lower than the unconditional benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: An upward turn in RSAFS indicates improving nominal consumer spending, supporting revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when sales growth is driven by inflation rather than real demand because margins then do not improve.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS growth does not predict above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative across two consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO implies stronger industrial activity and operating leverage for cyclical firms, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when production is rising only because of inventory rebuilds without final demand support.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum does not produce higher 60d SPX returns than flat or negative momentum states, or if the rank correlation is near zero over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST breaks above its recent trend while RSAFS is also accelerating, the combination identifies broad housing-led demand strength that supports earnings and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. Either series alone is insufficient because the effect depends on simultaneous real-economy demand confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive signal does not outperform either standalone proxy and fails to generate positive excess 60d returns in triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS regime above 4.5% tightens financial conditions and raises the discount rate on future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect is strongest when inflation is still elevated and policy remains restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes do not show lower 5d SPX returns than low-rate episodes, or if the interaction with CPIAUCSL fails to improve hit rate relative to FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE is rising, payroll deterioration confirms a labor-market downturn that should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if payroll weakness is isolated and unemployment stays stable.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change < 0 and UNRATE rises >= 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll declines and unemployment rises do not lead to negative 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the combined signal has no edge over either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in CPIAUCSL combined with firmer real activity in GDPC1 indicates disinflation without demand collapse, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through lower real yields and stable earnings expectations. The signal fails if disinflation is accompanied by contracting output.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation trend declines and GDPC1 is flat-to-positive versus prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation-plus-stable-output episodes do not precede positive 60d SPX returns, or if the signal turns negative during soft-landing quarters.
